bill.constitutionalAuthorityStatementText: [Congressional Record Volume 172, Number 94 (Wednesday, June 3, 2026)][House]From the Congressional Record Online through the Government Publishing Office [www.gpo.gov]By Mr. MOOLENAAR:H.R. 9129.Congress has the power to enact this legislation pursuantto the following:Congress derives its authority to enact this legislationfrom Article I, Section 8, Clause 3 (the Commerce Clause),which grants Congress the power to regulate Commerce withforeign Nations, as this bill regulates the importation andcommercial use of robotic systems manufactured by entitiessubject to the ownership, control, or direction of thePeople's Republic of China.[Page H3830]

item.text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

bill.title: To provide for certain humanoid and quadruped robotics communications equipment or services to be placed on the covered list of the Federal Communications Commission, and for other purposes.

item.title: To provide for certain humanoid and quadruped robotics communications equipment or services to be placed on the covered list of the Federal Communications Commission, and for other purposes.
To provide for certain humanoid and quadruped robotics communications equipment or services to be placed on the covered list of the Federal Communications Commission, and for other purposes.

latestAction.text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.
dublinCore.dc:contributor: Congressional Research Service, Library of Congress
dublinCore.dc:description: This file contains bill summaries and statuses for federal legislation. A bill summary describes the most significant provisions of a piece of legislation and details the effects the legislative text may have on current law and federal programs. Bill summaries are authored by the Congressional Research Service (CRS) of the Library of Congress. As stated in Public Law 91-510 (2 USC 166 (d)(6)), one of the duties of CRS is "to prepare summaries and digests of bills and resolutions of a public general nature introduced in the Senate or House of Representatives". For more information, refer to the User Guide that accompanies this file.
